response: THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 1ST CIRCLE IS R, AND EACH NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THE RADIUSES OF THE CIRCLES ARE R, 1.1R, 1.21R, 1.331R, 1.4641R, 1.61051R, 1.771561R, AND 1.9487171R. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR², π(1.1R)², π(1.21R)², π(1.331R)², π(1.4641R)², π(1.61051R)², π(1.771561R)², AND π(1.9487171R)². THIS CAN BE WRITTEN AS πR²(1 + 1.21 + 1.4641 + 1.61051 + 1.771561 + 1.9487171 + 2.14358881 + 2.34592513). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR²(1 + 1.21 + 1.4641 + 1.61051 + 1.771561 + 1.9487171 + 2.14358881 + 2.34592513). THIS IS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS CALCULATED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE BY THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR
